openQuestions.text: Ground the design in the actual current dispatch paths (cli-opencode, orchestrate, deep-review contracts).
Review phase 012 benchmark-results.md for the exact GPT-5.5 enforcement-inconsistency evidence.

resolvedQuestions.text: Does the @opencode-ai/plugin SDK tool.execute.before hook expose or allow persisting session-scoped state?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes — in-process closure Map (3 sibling plugins) and external file (mk-goal.js) both proven
Same loop-executor identity (prompt-parsed) dispatched >=3 per session = block; N=2 = warn

keyFindings.finding: tool.execute.before exposes input.sessionID and input.callID per call
In-process closure Map<sessionID,...> is established pattern (3 plugins)
External file state keyed by sessionID proven (mk-goal.js)
In-process state is primary; external is defense-in-depth for edge cases
keyFindings.source: opencode run is one-shot; repeat detection needs same-process

keyFindings.finding: subagent_type is always 'general' — cannot discriminate loop executors
Agent identity parsed from prompt: Agent:@X / Deep Route: mode=X / mode=X
Threshold: N>=3 same-loop-executor dispatches per session = block
prompt-improver not in mode-registry.json — needs static set

ruledOutDirections.reason: session-scoped is the natural boundary; time windows add complexity
callID is unique-per-call, not correlated across calls
